$comment: Eliza-1 bundle EXTRAS (WS10 prep, last touched 2026-05-14).
These artifacts are NOT part of the base-v1 release bundle described by
ELIZA_1_GGUF_PLATFORM_PLAN.json. They are runtime-downloaded add-ons that
the runtime / arbiter pulls on demand the first time a capability is
requested. Their presence in the user's local cache is gated by:
(a) the device class meeting the resident-RAM headroom budget in
docs/eliza-1-pipeline/05-memory-budgets.md, and
(b) the user opting in to the capability (image-gen, OCR, person-detect)
via the desktop/mobile setup flow.
DO NOT add these as required_files on the bundle plan: shipping a 4 GB
diffusion GGUF inside the base-v1 bundle would push the 0_8b/2b tiers far
beyond their advertised on-disk footprint and break first-run UX on phones.
Publishing pipeline contract (asserted by validate-bundle-plan.mjs):
Every imagegen entry (default + every optional) MUST carry one of:
- `url` (HTTPS URL pointing at the runtime download target), or
- `staged: true` AND a `buildPlan` block describing how the artifact
is produced (tool, source, command). Staged entries are gated off
from runtime downloads until the build step lands.
A `sha256` field (hex string) is optional but recommended for any entry
that has a `url`; the installer verifies it before activating the asset.
The validator (eliza/scripts/validate-bundle-plan.mjs) reads this file
alongside ELIZA_1_GGUF_PLATFORM_PLAN.json and asserts every per-tier entry
has a corresponding catalog entry in
eliza/packages/shared/src/local-inference/catalog.ts (or surfaces the gap
as a WS2 / WS3 follow-up).
Image-gen GGUFs ride on stable-diffusion.cpp (or a comparable
GGUF-aware diffusion runtime). Sizes are estimates from public
GGUF quants of each model:
- sdxl-turbo Q4_0:    ~3.6 GB
- z-image-turbo Q4_K_M: ~3.4 GB (Apache-2.0)
- flux-1-schnell Q4_K_M: ~6.8 GB (Apache-2.0 weights, Schnell only)

optional.gateNote: Flagship phones / mid-range tablets only. Skip on <8 GB RAM.

buildPlan.notes: Z-Image-Turbo is published as safetensors weights. The GGUF quant is built in-house with sd-cpp's converter; CI bundle prep runs this on the linux-x64 build matrix and uploads the result to releases.elizaos.ai.

optional.gateNote: Higher quality, requires >=24 GB unified RAM or >=12 GB VRAM.

buildPlan.notes: Z-Image-Turbo is published as safetensors weights. The GGUF quant is built in-house with sd-cpp's converter; CI bundle prep runs this on the linux-x64 build matrix and uploads the result to releases.elizaos.ai.

optional.gateNote: Higher quality, requires >=24 GB unified RAM or >=12 GB VRAM.

buildPlan.notes: Z-Image-Turbo is published as safetensors weights. The GGUF quant is built in-house with sd-cpp's converter; CI bundle prep runs this on the linux-x64 build matrix and uploads the result to releases.elizaos.ai.

optional.gateNote: Long-context tier; image-gen is co-resident with the 27B text weights — flagship workstations only.

$comment: Vision mmproj entries are already listed per tier in
ELIZA_1_GGUF_PLATFORM_PLAN.json under required_files
(vision/mmproj-<tier>.gguf). This block records the size estimates so
the budget table and arbiter can plan resident memory.
Estimates are for Qwen3-VL mmproj at the per-tier matching quant.
`staged: true` marks tiers whose mmproj GGUF is built locally by the
publish pipeline (packages/training/scripts/manifest/
stage_eliza1_bundle_assets.py) rather than mirrored verbatim from a
standalone HF mmproj GGUF repo. The 0_8b and 2b tiers fall here because
Qwen3-VL does not publish standalone mmproj GGUFs at these parameter
counts — the projector lives inside the upstream Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct
multimodal archive, and the build step extracts it and re-emits it as
an mtmd-compatible GGUF at the per-tier quant (Q4_K_M for 0_8b, Q8_0
for 2b). The 4B+ tiers mirror an already-published standalone mmproj.
The validator (scripts/validate-bundle-plan.mjs) accepts a `staged: true`
entry here as the resolution path for a hasVision tier whose plan
required_files lists vision/mmproj-<tier>.gguf — i.e. the bundle plan
references the file and the build pipeline produces it. Neither path
treats an empty publish state as a silent fallback.

stagedBuild.rationale: Qwen3-VL does not ship a native 0.8B projector. The 0_8b tier reuses the Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct projector requantized to Q4_K_M to keep the resident footprint under the 2 GB-floor mobile target (~220 MB on disk, ~270 MB resident). The runtime treats the projector as model-family `qwen3-vl` regardless of decoder size; this shared-projector pattern is consistent with how mtmd encodes are decoupled from the text decoder weights.

The 2B tier's mmproj is the upstream Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct projector exported as a standalone GGUF at Q8_0. The build step extracts the projector tensors from the upstream HF multimodal archive and re-emits them in mtmd-compatible GGUF; this is `staged` rather than `mirrored` because the upstream HF repo packages the projector inside the multimodal model checkpoint, not as a standalone mmproj GGUF.

$comment: OCR ships with the app, not the model bundle. RapidOCR PP-OCRv5 mobile
(detector + recognizer ONNX) is bundled by the desktop installer and
linked into the runtime workspace at first launch. Total resident
footprint is ~80 MB (det ~5 MB, rec ~75 MB).
Lightweight person/object detector for camera-to-reaction flows.
YOLOv8n / YOLOv11n at int8 ONNX is ~12 MB; ships with the app like OCR.

quantization.$comment: Image-gen quant config sidecar that mirrors the per-tier defaults.
